receipt: torrent stack on real hardware — verified manifest resolution + verified pull + standby seeding + verified peer pull
what_this_proves: The P0-#1 launch story 'torrent from peers, not the mirror' works end-to-end on REAL cross-WAN hardware with REAL MiniMax-M2.5-NVFP4 weights (10GB / 2 layers), through the signed-manifest verification path. First real-hardware exercise of the 2026-07-20/21 manifest+seed stack (prior P2P proof #61 was 5GB synthetic, not through shard.fetch's signed-manifest gates).

sidecar_release.verified_from_box: both boxes downloaded the PUBLISHED release and sha-matched the baked pin — the all-joins blocker fix works from a real box

stage1_verified_mirror_pull.trust_surface: --manifest-cid (bytes==CID) + --pubkey (ed25519 signature) + --expect-model-id + --expect-layer-count — ALL gates cleared before any byte, then every shard re-hashed vs the manifest (0 .part remaining)

stage2_seed_and_verified_peer_pull.seeder_A: sidecar -seed manifest=dir -> 'SEEDING 5 shards'; SERVED bytes[0:4961488672] + bytes[0:4998528136] + bytes[0:19521473] to B over libp2p
stage2_seed_and_verified_peer_pull.puller_B: shard.fetch --bootstrap <A addr> --mirror <DELIBERATELY BROKEN http://127.0.0.1:1/nope/> -> SHARD_FETCH_DONE {files:5, bytes:9979547922}; 'block verified: 5 files'
stage2_seed_and_verified_peer_pull.proof: the broken mirror means all 10GB came from peer A over libp2p and hash-verified against the signed manifest — verified peer delivery, not a mirror fallback

not_covered_here: full daemon integration (worker self-provision -> announce -> assign -> serve -> churn -> warm re-join) is npm-publish-gated (@c0mpute/worker still 2.8.2) + needs the full 62-layer model (6+ boxes); its control-plane logic is sim-proven (churn-proof.sh, p11-restart-test.sh, leg8-serve-test). Challenge-probe-on-GPU needs the sm_120 torch build (engine deps), skipped (the #126 sketch fix is test-proven by construction).
